title: Guam: Where America’s 250th Birthday Will First Be Honored
author: Frank F. Blas, Jr.
text:
U.S. service members and the local community plant flags to remember fallen U.S. troops at the War in the Pacific National Historical Park, Asan Beach, Guam, May 24, 2025.
Credit: U.S. Navy photo by Mass Communication Specialist 2nd Class Janae McCoy
When the sun rises on July 4, 2026, it will shine on Guam before reaching any other American soil. Long before fireworks light the skies over Washington, Philadelphia, or Boston, the first American community to greet the nation’s 250th birthday will be a small island in the western Pacific, an island that has given more to the United States than most Americans will ever know.“Where America’s Day Begins” is not a slogan crafted for tourism. It is a reflection of Guam’s place in the American story: geographically distant, strategically essential, and unwaveringly loyal. As the United States marks its semiquincentennial, Guam will once again stand at the front of the national timeline, and it is time the rest of the country recognizes what that truly means.[...]
